data.title: 'Time to Rotate Back into Mag 7 Stocks,' Says HSBC
data.description:
Investor fears about big tech and hyperscalers may be overblown, according to Max Kettner, chief multi-asset strategist at HSBC (HSBC). He believes that the recent market selloff has gone too far, especially when you look at how profitable these companies still are. For example, the largest stocks in the S&P 500 (SPY), including Nvidia (NVDA), Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOGL), Meta (META), Broadcom (AVGO), Tesla (TSLA), and Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B), now generate a combined return on equity of about 65%, compared to just 15% in 2017–2018.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Kettner argues that valuations now look unusually attractive. For instance, Amazon is trading near its lowest price-to-earnings ratio in 20 years. Meanwhile, the "Magnificent Seven" hyperscalers (MAGS) trade at around 26 times earnings, which is very close to the Russell 2000 (IWM) at roughly 25 times, despite the fact that nearly half of the Russell 2000 companies have negative earnings. Because of this mismatch, HSBC believes investors may have overreacted.

As a result, the firm recommends rotating out of high-beta momentum stocks and back into the Mag Seven, while also adding exposure to parts of the economy that could benefit from improving consumer strength. Specifically, HSBC favors homebuilders, regional banks, transportation ETFs, and retail funds. The reasoning is that these sectors could benefit from large tax refunds that are expected this year. Kettner also believes that economists have consistently underestimated the strength of U.S. consumers.

Which Magnificent Seven Stock Is the Better Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the Magnificent 7 stocks mentioned above, analysts think that Microsoft stock has the most room to run. In fact, Microsoft's average price target of $593.38 per share implies 48% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 'Don't Believe the Hype,' Says Investor as Nvidia Stock Faces a Reality Check
data.description:
The supercharged growth of Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) has been one of the defining market stories of the past few years. Unless you've been living under a rock, you know its GPUs sit at the heart of the AI revolution, powering the vast majority of compute capacity in data centers worldwide.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Its revenue and margins have become the stuff of legend, and the company continues to break sales records quarter after quarter. Many expect its upcoming Q4 FY2026 financial results, due on February 25, to continue this lucrative trend, especially as hyperscalers double down on their AI capex spending in 2026.

Indeed, a huge chunk of this cash will likely find its way into Nvidia's coffers. Yet, with NVDA shares largely moving sideways over the past six months, does this pause present a timely entry point before the next leg higher?

Not according to one investor, known by the pseudonym Oriental Trader, who believes that even after trading sideways, NVDA is simply too expensive. "The market is pricing NVDA to perfection to infinity," emphasizes the investor.

Oriental argues that Nvidia's lofty valuation hinges on one key premise – that hyperscalers will keep pouring massive sums into AI infrastructure year after year. If that spending ever cools, even a little, the whole thesis starts to wobble.

And that's where things get interesting. Oriental points out that the very companies driving that AI buildout – Microsoft, Meta, Amazon, Alphabet, and Oracle – have all pulled back from their all-time highs, suggesting investors may already be questioning how long this spending boom can really last.

The investor argues that the market is punishing the massive capex moves, and predicts that this will cause management to call into question the cost-benefit analysis of continuing to spend at such an astronomical rate.

"Recent stock price declines among major tech companies make a broad AI capex slowdown increasingly likely, threatening NVDA's growth narrative," adds Oriental.

Moreover, despite NVDA's lagging share price of late, the investor posits that it remains elevated with a forward price-to-earnings ratio of close to 40x. Oriental expects that multiple to come down once the expected capex crunch materializes.

And thus, concludes Oriental, the best way to take advantage of the AI revolution is to look beyond Nvidia.

"I see a diversified basket of major tech companies as a more prudent long-term bet than NVDA at current valuations," sums up Oriental, who rates NVDA a Strong Sell. (To watch Oriental Trader's track record, click here)

That's a decidedly contrarian view. On Wall Street, the overwhelming majority remain firmly in Nvidia's corner. With 37 Buys, just 1 Hold, and a single Sell, NVDA carries a Strong Buy consensus rating. Meanwhile, the 12-month average price target of $260.06 implies ~38% upside from current levels. (See NVDA stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Dow Jones Today: DJIA Boosted by Tech Strength ahead of Fed Minutes
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is trading higher on Wednesday as tech stocks lead the way ahead of the release of January's Fed minutes at 2 p.m. Eastern Time.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Every tech stock in the index is in the green, led by a 2% gain in Nvidia (NVDA). On Tuesday, the semiconductor leader announced that it would expand its partnership with Meta Platforms (META), which includes a commitment for millions of CPUs and Blackwell and Rubin GPUs.

"Through deep codesign across CPUs, GPUs, networking and software, we are bringing the full NVIDIA platform to Meta's researchers and engineers as they build the foundation for the next AI frontier," said Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang.

Ahead of the Fed minutes, the odds of a 25 bps interest rate cut at the next Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting on March 18 stand at a slim 5.9%. By the end of the year, however, traders expect two rate cuts, placing the federal funds rate between 3.00% and 3.25%.

Meanwhile, domestic production is on the rise. In January, U.S. industrial production increased by 0.7% from the prior month, marking the highest rate of growth since February 2025. On top of that, manufacturing activity, which accounts for roughly 75% of industrial production, increased by 0.6%, the highest in 11 months. Domestic production remains a key priority for the Trump administration, and with trade tensions easing and favorable tax policies in place, both domestic and foreign manufacturers are boosting investment in U.S. factories.

The Dow Jones is up by 0.59% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Housing Starts Leap to 5-Month High as Affordability Concerns Persist
data.description:
Homebuilders, like Lennar (LEN) and D.R. Horton (DHI), are back in business. In December, housing starts increased by 6.2%, the highest level in five months, to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 1.4 million homes. Single-family housing starts, which account for the majority of homebuilding, rose by 4.1% to an annualized rate of 981,000 homes.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In another positive sign for the homebuilding industry, building permits, which signal future construction activity, grew by 4.3% to an annualized rate of 1.45 million, registering the highest level since March.

Builder Confidence Slips as Buyers Remain Hesitant

Improving housing market conditions were likely behind the rise in both housing starts and building permits. Homebuilder sentiment ticked higher as 2025 came to a close, although confidence has since slipped as concerns mount over affordability.

"While the majority of builders continue to deploy buyer incentives, including price cuts, many prospective buyers remain on the sidelines," said National Association of Home Builders Chairman Buddy Hughes. Furthermore, buyers must still grapple with mortgage rates above 6%, while builders face elevated construction costs.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: 'Deal Risk Overblown,' Says Analyst as Netflix Stock Tests Investor Patience
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) shares have tumbled 42% since peaking last June, as what was once a high-flying story has turned into a tug-of-war over deal risk. The stock has come under mounting pressure amid concerns surrounding the proposed Warner Bros. acquisition, with investors increasingly uneasy that Netflix could be forced to sweeten its offer far more than originally expected.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, Rothschild & Co Redburn analyst Hamilton Faber thinks another bidding war is unlikely, and thinks the deal represents an opportunity for Netflix.

The analyst believes the acquisition could generate $0.6 billion, $1.1 billion, and $3.6 billion in revenue synergies over the first three years, driven by a bigger content lineup that attracts more subscribers, boosts engagement, and reduces churn, with extra gains if Netflix releases films in theaters.

Netflix expects that the deal will be accretive by the second year, but Faber says that doesn't account for these synergies, meaning actual EPS could be higher than expected. With synergies, Faber sees 10% EPS accretion in year two, rising to 22% in year three. Netflix's current business would still make up over 70% of pro forma EBITDA, and combined with synergies, the analyst estimates 2026–2029 EBITDA and EPS growth rates of 25% and 31%, compared with 19% and 22% if Netflix operated alone.

Additionally, Faber's sensitivity analysis indicates Paramount would struggle to outbid Netflix; even if Netflix increases its offer from $27.75 to $33.75, it only reduces 2029 accretion from 22% to 19%. "This implies it makes little sense for Paramount to bid higher," Faber said on the matter. 

But what about the prospect that the deal does not get regulatory approval? Faber thinks that is unlikely. The streaming market is highly fragmented and will stay that way even if Netflix acquires Warner Bros. The "most negative commentary" revolves around Netflix's high subscriber count post-merger, but they overlook that 80% of WB's subscribers already overlap with Netflix's (according to Co-CEO Ted Sarandos). The merger would keep Netflix as the global leader, with only an 8% subscriber increase to 393 million. Its higher subscriber count mainly reflects strong international growth, and Faber thinks the company should not be penalised for that.

But even if the deal doesn't get the green light and Netflix pivots back to its standalone strategy, Faber remains confident. "Either way," he says, "we believe the current price represents an attractive entry point… If Netflix does fail to acquire WB, we continue to see strong operating leverage and still forecast a 2025-29 EPS CAGR of 23%."

Accordingly, Faber assigns NFLX shares a Buy rating, backed by a $120 price target, implying the stock will climb 56% higher in the months ahead. (To watch Faber's track record, click here)

Among Faber's colleagues, 26 other analysts rate NFLX shares a Buy, while an additional 8 Holds and 1 Sell, add up to a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Going by the $114.52 average price target, the stock will gain 47% over the next year. (See NFLX stock forecast)
data.author: Marty Shtrubel

data.title: Nvidia (NVDA) Exits Its Arm Stake, but Not the Technology
data.description:
Shares of British chip designer Arm (ARM) are up on Wednesday after new filings showed that chipmaker Nvidia (NVDA) has fully exited its stake in the company. Notably, at the end of the third quarter, Nvidia owned about 1.1 million Arm shares worth roughly $155.8 million, but a Tuesday SEC filing confirmed that the position had been sold. Originally, Nvidia was part of a group of strategic investors that collectively bought $735 million worth of Arm shares during its 2023 Nasdaq debut.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

That IPO investment followed Nvidia's failed attempt to acquire Arm outright for $40 billion in 2020 from SoftBank (SFTBF). In fact, the deal collapsed in 2022 after facing regulatory opposition in both the U.S. and Europe. Despite selling its equity stake, Nvidia's relationship with Arm remains intact. After the takeover fell through, Nvidia retained its 20-year Arm license, with CEO Jensen Huang stating that the company would remain a long-term partner. Indeed, Nvidia's Grace CPUs, which it positions as a foundation for next-generation data centers, are built using Arm's chip architecture.

At the same time, Arm is continuing to grow on its own. In its latest quarter, revenue increased by 26% from a year ago to $1.24 billion, which was higher than analysts expected. Interestingly, Morgan Stanley (MS) said the results showed that there is strong momentum tied to AI demand, and that the company is spending more money to support future growth. While the stock slipped slightly after earnings because of cautious guidance, shares are still up 16% this year, and Morgan Stanley kept a Buy rating with a $135 price target.

Is ARM Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on ARM stock based on 18 Buys, four Holds, and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average ARM price target of $159.50 per share implies 23.4% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Ethereum Price Is Acting Like a Magnet for Institutional Giants as Big Money Eyes a $2,500 Rebound
data.description:
Ethereum (ETH-USD) is currently hovering near $1,974, still struggling to push past the major $2,500 mark. Even though the price fell as low as $1,744 earlier this month, the bigwigs of the financial world are not running away. Instead, they are positioning themselves for a comeback. From university endowments to the world's largest fund managers, the trend is that institutional money is looking at Ethereum's utility and staking rewards as a massive opportunity for 2026.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Elite University Funds Shift toward ETH

The Harvard Management Company, which looks after Harvard's $57 billion endowment, recently made a big move. New SEC filings show they bought $87 million worth of BlackRock's Ether ETF. During the same period, they cut their Bitcoin holdings by about 21%. This shift suggests that the smartest money on Wall Street is starting to see more value in the Ethereum ecosystem compared to the digital gold narrative of Bitcoin (BTC-USD).

BlackRock Adds a Bonus to its New ETF

BlackRock (BLK) is upgrading its Ethereum offerings to include staking. Unlike a standard ETF that just tracks the price, this new version will actually earn rewards from the network. BlackRock recently clarified that they will keep an 18% fee from these rewards to pay for the tech and security, while investors get the other 82%. This change makes it much easier for regular people to earn interest on their crypto without managing the technical side themselves.

Big Banks Choose Security over Speed

Ethereum has become the undisputed king of Real-World Assets (RWA). Traditional assets worth over $15 billion, including gold, tokenized stocks, and U.S. Treasurys, now sit on the Ethereum blockchain.

This represents a 200% increase from last year. While other networks like Solana are faster, giants like JPMorgan (JPM) and BlackRock prefer Ethereum because it is the most secure. This massive interest from traditional finance creates a constant demand for ETH that could eventually push the price back toward the $2,500 level.

At the time of writing, Ether is sitting at $1,976.85.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Canada's Restaurant Brands International (QSR) Turns to Promotions to Boost Flagging Sales
data.description:
Canada's Restaurant Brands International (QSR), the parent company of Burger King and Tim Hortons, is turning to promotions to attract customers and boost its flagging sales.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Toronto-based company announced that it is bringing back a popular sweepstakes promotion at its Tim Hortons coffee and doughnut chain called "Roll Up The Rim To Win." The contest allows customers to win items ranging from a free coffee to a new car, and has proven popular in the past.

Tim Hortons made a significant change to the contest in 2020 during the COVID-19 pandemic, switching to a new digital sweepstakes format that proved unpopular with consumers. But now, in an effort to boost sales, Restaurant Brands is bringing back the original contest.

"We're thrilled to share that Rolling Up The Rim of our cups is coming back – starting on Feb. 23 – and not just for this year, but for years to come," said the company in a news release.

Restaurant Brands' Latest Print

This year's contest features more than 30 million prizes available to be won, including vacations, gift cards, electronics, and 17 million coffees. The contest kicks off on Feb. 23 and runs through March 22, according to a Tim Hortons news release.

A week ago, Restaurant Brands reported its latest financial results. While the company posted top and bottom line beats, its Tim Hortons unit was weak during the quarter. Tim Hortons posted same-store sales growth of 2.9%, below the 3.8% anticipated by analysts. Tim Hortons accounts for 46% of Restaurant Brands' annual revenue and is strategically important to the company.

Is QSR Stock a Buy?

Restaurant Brands International's stock has a consensus Moderate Buy rating among 19 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 11 Buy, seven Hold, and one Sell recommendations issued in the last three months. The average QSR price target of $77.06 implies 11.66% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Google Stock Prepares to Fire as it Debuts $499 Pixel 10a to Snuff Out Apple's iPhone 17e
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) shares moved slightly to $303.34 on Wednesday as the company revealed its new Pixel 10a. Launching the phone now allows Google to try to lock in budget buyers before Apple (AAPL) can release its own affordable model, the iPhone 17e. Google's plan is to keep the price at $499, which is $100 cheaper than what Apple is expected to charge. Preorders are open now, and the phones will start shipping on March 5.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Google Chooses AI Smarts over New Hardware

Google is not making big changes to how the phone looks, but it is making the software much smarter. The company added two high-end AI camera tools, Auto Best Take and Camera Coach, to this cheaper model. These features help fix group photos where someone blinked and give live tips on how to take a better picture. The phone also includes Satellite SOS for emergencies. While the screen is a bit brighter than last year, Google kept most of the hardware the same to make sure the price stayed low.

The most unique part of the Pixel 10a is its back. Unlike most modern phones, the camera does not stick out at all. This means the phone can lay perfectly flat on a table without wobbling. Maintaining the cost at $499 required Google to remove the "PixelSnap" magnetic charging found on its more expensive phones.

To sum up, Google is trying to pull a fast one on Apple. Releasing the Pixel 10a first with a lower price serves as a way to tempt people to switch. The phone isn't a massive upgrade in terms of raw power, but it has enough cool AI tricks to feel modern.

Is Google a Good Stock to Buy?

Alphabet's stock GOOGL continues to carry a Strong Buy consensus, based on 32 analyst ratings over the past three months. Out of those, 25 call it a Buy, while seven recommend a Hold. None of the analysts currently suggest selling.

The average 12-month GOOGL price target sits at $379.47, which represents 25% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani
